title: Introduction to Project Management
description: Fundamentals of project management including definitions, history, the role of project managers, and the PMBOK framework.

title: Project Performance Domains
description: The eight project performance domains that are critical for effective delivery of project outcomes, including stakeholder, team, development approach, planning, project work, delivery, measurement, and uncertainty.

title: Tailoring, Models, Methods & Artifacts
description: How to adapt project management approaches to fit the project context, and common models, methods, and artifacts used in project management.

title: PM in the Organization
description: How projects relate to organizational strategy, structure, and culture. Understanding the organizational context in which projects operate.

title: Team Management
description: Team development stages, building trust, managing conflict, embracing accountability, and leadership in project teams.

title: Risk Management
description: Identifying, analyzing, planning responses to, and monitoring project risks throughout the project lifecycle.

title: Estimation
description: Estimation methodologies including expert judgment, analogous estimation, parametric estimation, function points, and COCOMO.

title: Scheduling and Tracking
description: Work Breakdown Structures, network analysis, critical path method, Gantt charts, and project tracking techniques.

title: Quality Management
description: Software quality concepts, quality assurance, quality control, testing strategies, and measurement metrics.

title: Stakeholder Management
description: Identifying stakeholders, stakeholder analysis, communication management, and engagement strategies.

title: Change Management
description: Integrated change control, configuration management, software maintenance, and managing project changes systematically.
